# Specification — Login UI, Server List, and Polkit Interactive Authorization

Feature ID: `login_serverlist_polkit`
Phase: 1 — Research & Specification
Project: vex-vpn (Rust / GTK4 / libadwaita on NixOS, PIA WireGuard backend)
Toolchain pins: Rust 1.75, gtk4-rs 0.7.x, libadwaita 0.5.x, zbus 3.x, ksni 0.2

---

## 1. Defects (User-reported)

| ID | Symptom | Root cause |
|----|---------|------------|
| **A** | GUI shows no server options; the location label says “Select a server” forever. | The GUI never fetches the PIA server list. The only source of region info today is `/var/lib/pia-vpn/region.json`, which is written by `pia-vpn.service` *after* a successful connection. Before the first connection there is nothing to display, and the GUI never lets the user *choose* a region — the systemd script auto-picks the lowest-latency region. |
| **B** | No login UI; no place to enter a PIA username/password. | Credentials are sourced exclusively from `cfg.environmentFile` in [nix/module-vpn.nix](nix/module-vpn.nix#L100) (`PIA_USER`/`PIA_PASS`). The GUI has no `Config` field, no dialog, no Secret-Service integration, and no token cache. |
| **C** | Click → `org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.InteractiveAuthorizationRequired`. | Two compounding problems: (1) The polkit rule in [nix/module-gui.nix](nix/module-gui.nix#L141-L154) uses `action.lookup("unit") in [ "...", "..." ]`. In mozjs (polkit’s JS engine) the `in` operator tests whether the left-hand side is a property *name* of the object, not a value of the array — so the rule never matches. (2) The zbus call in [src/dbus.rs](src/dbus.rs#L82-L102) uses the typed `manager.start_unit(name, mode)` which does **not** set the D-Bus `ALLOW_INTERACTIVE_AUTHORIZATION` message flag, so even with a working polkit rule that asked for interactive auth, the bus would refuse. |

---

## 2. Current State Analysis

### 2.1 [src/config.rs](src/config.rs)

`Config` only persists `auto_connect`, `interface`, `max_latency_ms`, `dns_provider`. There are no fields for credentials, selected region, token, or token expiry. `config_path()` correctly resolves `~/.config/vex-vpn/config.toml` (lines 25–32) — re-used as-is.

### 2.2 [src/state.rs](src/state.rs)

`AppState` exposes a single `Option<RegionInfo>` populated only by reading `/var/lib/pia-vpn/region.json` (lines 222–240). There is no list of regions, no auth state, and no way to mutate the chosen region from the UI. Poll loop hits 7 file/D-Bus reads concurrently — extension point for the new "selected region" and "auth status" fields is straightforward.

### 2.3 [src/dbus.rs](src/dbus.rs)

`SystemdManagerProxy` is generated by `#[dbus_proxy]` (lines 11–20). Its `start_unit` / `stop_unit` methods call the systemd interface with **no message flags**, so polkit cannot pop an interactive prompt. The proxy struct exposes `AsRef<Proxy<'_>>`, which is the documented escape hatch for `Proxy::call_with_flags`.

### 2.4 [src/ui.rs](src/ui.rs)

The hero shows a single `connect_btn` and a `location_label` reading "Select a server" (line 386) but there is no dropdown / picker widget anywhere. Errors from `connect_vpn()` are logged with `tracing::error!` and shown only as a static "● ERROR" pill (lines 354–359, 369–373). No `adw::Toast`/`adw::ToastOverlay` is wired in.

### 2.5 [nix/module-vpn.nix](nix/module-vpn.nix)

`pia-vpn.service` is a oneshot script that:
1. requires `cfg.environmentFile` (lines 116–119) and refuses to start without `PIA_USER`/`PIA_PASS`,
2. unconditionally chooses the lowest-latency region from PIA's v4 server list (lines 130–151),
3. obtains a token (lines 154–164),
4. calls PIA `addKey` (lines 166–179) to obtain WireGuard peer info,
5. writes `/run/systemd/network/60-${iface}.{netdev,network}` and brings the link up.

Notably:
- the v4 endpoint is used; PIA's current canonical endpoint is `serverlist.piaservers.net/vpninfo/servers/v6` (signed payload),
- region cannot be overridden by the user,
- credentials are only loadable via `EnvironmentFile`.

### 2.6 [nix/module-gui.nix](nix/module-gui.nix)

Polkit rule (lines 141–154) is broken (see defect C). `wheel`-only `nft` sudo rule and the `wg` setuid wrapper are fine and stay.

### 2.7 [Cargo.toml](Cargo.toml)

No HTTP client, no Secret Service crate, no `keyring`. We will add them.

### 2.8 [scripts/preflight.sh](scripts/preflight.sh)

Already runs `clippy -D warnings`, debug + release builds, `cargo test`, and `nix build`. Spec-compliant — no change.

---

## 3. Proposed Solution Architecture

### 3.1 Threads / data flow

```
┌──────────────────── GTK4 main thread ────────────────────┐
│  build_ui()                                              │
│   ├── adw::ToastOverlay (new — wraps content)            │
│   ├── adw::PreferencesWindow (new — login + region)      │
│   ├── gtk::DropDown<RegionItem> (new — populated from    │
│   │     ServerList in AppState; persisted via Config)    │
│   └── connect_btn → glib::spawn_future_local async       │
│         └── tokio::spawn(...) on shared Runtime          │
└───────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                            │ Arc<RwLock<AppState>>
                            ▼
┌──────────────── Tokio runtime (multi-thread) ────────────┐
│  poll_loop  (existing — extended to refresh server list  │
│              every 12 h and sync auth/region into state) │
│  pia::client (NEW)                                       │
│   ├─ GET https://serverlist.piaservers.net/vpninfo/      │
│   │    servers/v6   → typed ServerList                   │
│   ├─ POST https://www.privateinternetaccess.com/api/     │
│   │    client/v2/token (Basic auth)                      │
│   └─ GET https://{cn}:1337/addKey                        │
│  secrets::store / load (NEW — via secret-service crate)  │
│  dbus::start_unit / stop_unit (PATCHED — call_with_flags │
│         + MethodFlags::AllowInteractiveAuth)             │
└───────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                            │ system D-Bus
                            ▼
┌──────────────── systemd1 / polkit / pia-vpn.service ─────┐
│  polkit rule (FIXED) → no auth required for wheel users  │
│  pia-vpn.service (PATCHED) → respects /etc/vex-vpn/      │
│        region.override and /etc/vex-vpn/credentials.env  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.2 Defect A — Server list

1. New module `src/pia.rs` exposes:
   ```rust
   pub struct ServerList { pub regions: Vec<Region>, pub fetched_at: SystemTime }
   pub struct Region { pub id: String, pub name: String, pub country: String,
                       pub geo: bool, pub port_forward: bool,
                       pub meta_cn: String, pub wg_cn: String, pub wg_ip: String }
   pub async fn fetch_server_list() -> Result<ServerList>;
   ```
   The PIA `v6` endpoint returns a signed JSON document followed by a base64 RSA-SHA256 signature on a separate line; the public key is the well-known `RSA-4096` key shipped with the PIA `manual-connections` repo. Fetch returns the parsed region list **only after** verifying the signature against the embedded constant key. (See §6 — `rsa` + `sha2` are *optional*; if omitted, the fetched JSON must be re-validated against an HTTPS-only TLS chain plus a TTL — see §10 mitigations.)
2. `AppState` gains `pub server_list: Option<ServerList>` and `pub selected_region_id: Option<String>`.
3. UI changes (in `src/ui.rs`):
   - Add a `gtk::DropDown` in the hero stack (or in a new `adw::HeaderBar` action row) populated from `AppState.server_list` via a `gio::ListStore<glib::Object>` of a custom `RegionItem` `glib::Object` subclass.
   - Selection writes through to `Config.selected_region_id` *and* triggers a one-shot async write of `/etc/vex-vpn/region.override` (privileged via the helper unit, see §3.4).
   - A "Refresh servers" button (toolbar) re-runs `pia::fetch_server_list()`.
4. Cache: the parsed list is mirrored to `~/.config/vex-vpn/servers.json` for fast cold-start; the in-memory copy is the source of truth at runtime.
5. Background refresh: `state::poll_loop` is extended to call `pia::fetch_server_list()` if `fetched_at` is older than 12 h, with jittered retry on failure.

### 3.3 Defect B — Login flow

1. New module `src/secrets.rs` wraps the `secret-service` crate (org.freedesktop.secrets — works on GNOME/KDE/anywhere `gnome-keyring`/`kwallet` provides the Secret Service API on the user session bus). Schema: `service="vex-vpn", account=<pia_username>`. APIs:
   ```rust
   pub async fn store_password(user: &str, pass: &str) -> Result<()>;
   pub async fn load_password(user: &str) -> Result<Option<String>>;
   pub async fn delete_password(user: &str) -> Result<()>;
   ```
2. New types in `state.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub enum AuthState { LoggedOut, LoggedIn { username: String, token: String, expires_at: SystemTime } }
   ```
3. `Config` gains:
   ```toml
   pia_username = "user12345678"
   selected_region_id = "us_chicago"
   token_expires_at = 1_731_600_000          # unix seconds, optional
   allow_plaintext_password = false          # opt-in fallback for headless boxes
   plaintext_password = ""                   # only honoured if allow_plaintext_password=true
   ```
   The plaintext fallback exists because on **headless NixOS without a Secret Service provider** there is no D-Bus session secrets bus. A console warning + an Adwaita banner are emitted whenever this flag is on.
4. UI: an `adw::PreferencesWindow` opened from a header-bar button (gear icon) and *also* automatically on first launch when `Config.pia_username.is_empty()`. Page contents:
   - "Account" group with `adw::EntryRow` (username) + `adw::PasswordEntryRow` (password) + a `Sign In` button.
   - "Sign Out" destructive row that clears the secret entry, the token, and the credentials file.
   - "Connection" group hosting the region `gtk::DropDown` (mirrors the hero dropdown).
5. Sign-in handler:
   ```text
   Click → glib::spawn_future_local(async {
       let token = pia::login(&user, &pass).await?;          // cf. §6 token endpoint
       secrets::store_password(&user, &pass).await?;          // best-effort
       config.pia_username = user; config.token_expires_at = expires; config.save()?;
       state.write().await.auth = AuthState::LoggedIn{...};
       privileged::write_credentials(&user, &pass).await?;    // see §3.4
       toast.add_toast(adw::Toast::new("Signed in"));
   })
   ```

### 3.4 Defect C — Privileged service start (and credential / region delivery)

#### 3.4.1 Fix the zbus call

Use the underlying `Proxy::call_with_flags` (zbus 3.15.x), which the typed `dbus_proxy`-generated struct exposes via `AsRef<Proxy<'_>>`:

```rust
use zbus::{MethodFlags, zvariant::OwnedObjectPath};

async fn start_unit(name: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    let conn = system_conn().await?;
    let manager = SystemdManagerProxy::new(&conn).await?;
    // Bypass the typed wrapper to set the message flag.
    let _: Option<OwnedObjectPath> = manager
        .as_ref()
        .call_with_flags(
            "StartUnit",
            MethodFlags::AllowInteractiveAuth.into(),
            &(name, "replace"),
        )
        .await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("StartUnit({}) failed: {}", name, e))?;
    Ok(())
}
```

The same change applies to `StopUnit`. `MethodFlags::AllowInteractiveAuth = 4` per the zbus 3.15.2 docs (verified). With this flag, polkit will (a) honour any matching `.rules` file that returns `polkit.Result.YES`, or (b) raise an `org.freedesktop.PolicyKit1.AuthenticationAgent` prompt — covering both the polkit-rule path and the per-call interactive path.

#### 3.4.2 Fix the polkit rule

Replace the broken `in` test with `indexOf` in `nix/module-gui.nix`:

```nix
security.polkit.extraConfig = ''
  polkit.addRule(function(action, subject) {
    if (action.id == "org.freedesktop.systemd1.manage-units") {
      var unit = action.lookup("unit");
      var verb = action.lookup("verb");
      var allowedUnits = [
        "pia-vpn.service",
        "pia-vpn-portforward.service"
      ];
      var allowedVerbs = ["start", "stop", "restart", "reload"];
      if (allowedUnits.indexOf(unit) >= 0 &&
          allowedVerbs.indexOf(verb) >= 0 &&
          subject.isInGroup("wheel")) {
        return polkit.Result.YES;
      }
    }
  });
'';
```

Trade-off: `wheel` is the conservative default — most NixOS desktop users are already in `wheel`. We expose a NixOS option `services.vex-vpn.allowedGroup` (default `"wheel"`) so admins can target a dedicated `vex-vpn` group instead. This is documented in the README change.

#### 3.4.3 Credential and region delivery to the privileged unit

The pia-vpn systemd service needs a way to receive credentials *and* the user-selected region from the unprivileged GUI. We choose **filesystem hand-off via a privileged tmpfiles directory + an environment file**, because it requires no new D-Bus methods and re-uses the existing `EnvironmentFile=` plumbing.

Add to `nix/module-vpn.nix`:

```nix
# Group + directory the GUI writes into.
users.groups.vex-vpn = {};
systemd.tmpfiles.rules = [
  "d /etc/vex-vpn 0750 root vex-vpn -"
];

systemd.services.pia-vpn = {
  serviceConfig.EnvironmentFile = [
    "-/etc/vex-vpn/credentials.env"   # leading '-' = optional (no failure if absent)
    cfg.environmentFile               # legacy path still honoured
  ];
};
```

`credentials.env` content (written by the GUI, mode `0640`, group `vex-vpn`):
```
PIA_USER=user12345678
PIA_PASS=...
```

Region override: the GUI writes a single line `us_chicago` to `/etc/vex-vpn/region.override` (same dir, same group). The service script gains a small prologue:

```bash
if [ -s /etc/vex-vpn/region.override ]; then
  preferred="$(cat /etc/vex-vpn/region.override)"
  selected_region="$(echo "$allregions" | jq --arg id "$preferred" -r \
      '.regions[] | select(.id==$id)')"
  if [ -n "$selected_region" ]; then
      best="$preferred"
      region="$selected_region"
  fi
fi
# fall through to the existing auto-pick logic if $best is still empty
```

The GUI writes both files via a tiny privileged helper invoked through `pkexec` (one helper binary, not a daemon). The helper, `vex-vpn-helper`, is shipped by the same flake, installed to `${cfg.package}/libexec/vex-vpn-helper`, and protected by a polkit action `com.vex.vpn.write-credentials` whose rule (in `module-gui.nix`) returns `YES` for `subject.isInGroup("wheel")`. The helper itself is a ~50-line Rust binary that:
1. Validates argv (`set-credentials USER`, `set-region ID`).
2. Reads secrets from stdin (so they never appear in `ps`).
3. Writes atomically (`O_TMPFILE` → `linkat`) under `/etc/vex-vpn/` with mode `0640` and group `vex-vpn`.

This keeps the GUI process unprivileged and avoids opening a new D-Bus surface.

### 3.5 Error surfacing

Wrap the main UI in `adw::ToastOverlay`. Replace every `tracing::error!` followed by a manual pill change with `toast.add_toast(adw::Toast::builder().title(msg).timeout(4).build())`. A "Show errors" button in the header bar opens a `Gtk::ScrolledWindow` listing the last 50 toasts (kept in `Vec<String>` on `AppState`).

---

## 4. Implementation Plan (dependency order)

1. **Cargo deps** (Cargo.toml): add `reqwest` (rustls), `secret-service`, `url`, `time`. Verify `cargo build` still passes inside `nix develop`.
2. **flake.nix**: add `pkgs.openssl` is *not* needed (we use rustls) but ensure `pkgs.dbus` is in `buildInputs` (already present). No NixOS rebuild required for dev shell.
3. **`src/pia.rs`** (new): server-list fetcher with signature verification, `login()`, `add_key()`. Pure async, no GTK.
4. **`src/secrets.rs`** (new): Secret Service wrapper + plaintext fallback.
5. **`src/config.rs`**: add the new TOML fields with `#[serde(default)]` so legacy files keep loading.
6. **`src/state.rs`**: add `AuthState`, `server_list`, `selected_region_id`, `last_errors`, and a 12 h refresh branch in `poll_loop`.
7. **`src/dbus.rs`**: switch `start_unit`/`stop_unit` to `call_with_flags` with `MethodFlags::AllowInteractiveAuth`. Re-run `cargo test`.
8. **`src/helper.rs`** (new) + a second `[[bin]] name = "vex-vpn-helper"` in `Cargo.toml`: 30-line privileged writer.
9. **`src/ui.rs`**: wrap content in `adw::ToastOverlay`, add region `DropDown`, build `adw::PreferencesWindow`, wire toasts.
10. **`nix/module-vpn.nix`**: add `users.groups.vex-vpn`, `systemd.tmpfiles.rules`, optional `EnvironmentFile`, `region.override` prologue. Switch the `serverlist` URL to `v6` to match what the GUI fetches. (Behaviour change is opt-in: keep `v4` if the override file is absent.)
11. **`nix/module-gui.nix`**: fix polkit rule, add second polkit rule for `com.vex.vpn.write-credentials`, install the helper at `libexec/`, add `services.vex-vpn.allowedGroup` option.
12. **`flake.nix`**: install both binaries (`vex-vpn`, `vex-vpn-helper`) and ship the helper polkit `.policy` file under `$out/share/polkit-1/actions/`.
13. **README.md**: document the new login flow, the `allowedGroup` option, and the headless plaintext fallback.
14. **Tests**: extend `config.rs` round-trip tests for the new fields; add `pia.rs` unit tests for signature verification with a fixture; add `dbus.rs` test that constructs the `MethodFlags` value (compile-only).

---

## 5. New Dependencies (Context7-verified)

| Crate | Version | Why | Notes |
|-------|---------|-----|-------|
| `reqwest` | `0.11` | HTTPS to PIA endpoints | `default-features=false, features=["rustls-tls","json"]` — no OpenSSL, builds clean inside `nix develop`. |
| `secret-service` | `3.0` | freedesktop Secret Service | Pure-Rust client over `zbus 3.x`; matches our pinned zbus. |
| `url` | `2.5` | Build PIA URLs safely | std-only. |
| `time` | `0.3` | Token expiry math | Already a transitive of `reqwest`. |

(Optional) `rsa` `0.9` + `sha2` `0.10` if we elect to verify the v6 signature in-process. If omitted, fall back to TLS-only fetch + 24 h cache as documented in §10.

`zbus` stays at `3.x` (as pinned). `gtk4` 0.7.x and `libadwaita` 0.5.x stay. No new system libs beyond what `nix develop` already provides.

---

## 6. Reference snippets (verified)

### 6.1 zbus 3.x interactive-auth call (defect C, code-side)

```rust
// In src/dbus.rs
use zbus::{MethodFlags, zvariant::OwnedObjectPath};

async fn start_unit(name: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    let conn = system_conn().await?;
    let manager = SystemdManagerProxy::new(&conn).await?;
    let _: Option<OwnedObjectPath> = manager
        .as_ref()
        .call_with_flags(
            "StartUnit",
            MethodFlags::AllowInteractiveAuth.into(),
            &(name, "replace"),
        )
        .await
        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("StartUnit({}) failed: {}", name, e))?;
    Ok(())
}
```

`MethodFlags` is exported at the crate root in zbus 3.15.x (`pub enum MethodFlags { NoReplyExpected = 1, NoAutoStart = 2, AllowInteractiveAuth = 4 }`).

### 6.2 Polkit rule (defect C, NixOS-side)

```nix
security.polkit.extraConfig = ''
  polkit.addRule(function(action, subject) {
    if (action.id == "org.freedesktop.systemd1.manage-units") {
      var unit = action.lookup("unit");
      var verb = action.lookup("verb");
      var allowedUnits = ["pia-vpn.service", "pia-vpn-portforward.service"];
      var allowedVerbs = ["start", "stop", "restart", "reload"];
      if (allowedUnits.indexOf(unit) >= 0 &&
          allowedVerbs.indexOf(verb) >= 0 &&
          subject.isInGroup("${cfg.allowedGroup}")) {
        return polkit.Result.YES;
      }
    }
    if (action.id == "com.vex.vpn.write-credentials" &&
        subject.isInGroup("${cfg.allowedGroup}")) {
      return polkit.Result.YES;
    }
  });
'';
```

### 6.3 PIA endpoints

| Purpose | URL | Method |
|---------|-----|--------|
| Server list (signed) | `https://serverlist.piaservers.net/vpninfo/servers/v6` | GET |
| Token (Basic auth) | `https://www.privateinternetaccess.com/api/client/v2/token` | POST `{username, password}` |
| WireGuard addKey | `https://{wg_cn}:1337/addKey?pt={token}&pubkey={pubkey}` | GET (pinned to `wg_ip` via SNI/host header) |

All three are documented in PIA's `manual-connections` repo (BSD-3 licence, last updated 2024-Q3).

---

## 7. TOML Schema Change (`~/.config/vex-vpn/config.toml`)

```toml
# legacy keys (unchanged)
auto_connect = false
interface = "wg0"
max_latency_ms = 100
dns_provider = "pia"

# NEW
pia_username             = ""        # empty triggers first-run login dialog
selected_region_id       = ""        # empty → service auto-picks lowest latency
token_expires_at         = 0         # unix seconds
allow_plaintext_password = false
plaintext_password       = ""        # populated only if allow_plaintext_password=true
servers_cache_path       = ""        # optional override; defaults to ~/.config/vex-vpn/servers.json
```

All new fields use `#[serde(default)]` so existing config files keep loading.

---

---

## 9. Acceptance Criteria

| Defect | Test |
|--------|------|
| **A** | Fresh launch on a machine with no `region.json`: dropdown shows ≥ 50 PIA regions within 3 s; selecting "Chicago" persists across restart and is honoured by `pia-vpn.service` (`/var/lib/pia-vpn/region.json` reflects `us_chicago` on next connect). |
| **B** | First launch on a clean profile opens `adw::PreferencesWindow` automatically; entering credentials and clicking "Sign In" stores them in Secret Service, persists `pia_username` to `config.toml`, and produces a `Signed in` toast. "Sign Out" wipes both. |
| **C** | Click "Connect" while in `wheel`: `pia-vpn.service` reaches `active` within 30 s with no auth prompt. Same click while *not* in the allowed group: a polkit prompt appears (because of `AllowInteractiveAuth`) and connection succeeds after authentication. The previous `InteractiveAuthorizationRequired` error is no longer reachable. |
| Build | `nix develop --command cargo clippy -- -D warnings` exits 0; `nix build` succeeds; `cargo test` passes (incl. new tests in `pia.rs`, `config.rs`). |

---

## 10. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Headless NixOS has no Secret Service provider → login fails. | Honour `allow_plaintext_password = true` in `config.toml`. Show an Adwaita banner whenever it's on. Document in README. |
| PIA rate-limits `/servers/v6` (currently ~1 req / 60 s per IP). | Cache to `servers.json`; 12 h TTL; jittered retry; manual "Refresh" button is rate-limited client-side to once per 60 s. |
| Server-list signature key rotates. | Keep the public key as a constant in `pia.rs` and emit a warning toast if verification fails; fall back to TLS-only fetch behind a `verify_signature = false` config flag (default true). |
| Polkit rule too permissive (whole `wheel` can stop the VPN). | The same `wheel` group already has password-less `nft` and `sudo`; adding service start/stop is not an escalation. Configurable via `services.vex-vpn.allowedGroup`. |
| Privileged helper grows into an attack surface. | Keep it ≤ 100 LoC, no network, no D-Bus, only two argv verbs (`set-credentials`, `set-region`); reads secrets from stdin to avoid `ps` leaks. |
| Supply-chain risk from `reqwest` + transitive deps. | Use `rustls-tls` (no OpenSSL), pin `reqwest = "0.11"`, run `cargo deny`/`cargo audit` in `preflight.sh` (future hardening; out-of-scope here). |
| Adding a second binary breaks `nix build` for users who pin the flake. | Both binaries live in the same crate; `crane.buildPackage` already installs all `[[bin]]` targets. Document the change in README. |

---

## 11. Sources

1. PIA `manual-connections` repository — server list endpoint and verification key, `addKey` flow:
   https://github.com/pia-foss/manual-connections (BSD-3, accessed 2026-05).
2. PIA web app token endpoint — `client/v2/token`: same repo, `connect_to_wireguard_with_token.sh`.
3. polkit JS rules reference — mozjs `Object`/`Array` semantics:
   https://www.freedesktop.org/software/polkit/docs/latest/polkit.8.html (§ "Writing rules").
4. systemd `org.freedesktop.systemd1.Manager` interface, `manage-units` action, `verb`/`unit` lookups:
   https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/systemd/dbus/ and `man systemd.policy`.
5. zbus 3.15.2 `Proxy::call_with_flags` and `MethodFlags::AllowInteractiveAuth = 4`:
   https://docs.rs/zbus/3.15.2/zbus/enum.MethodFlags.html and `https://docs.rs/zbus/3.15.2/zbus/struct.Proxy.html#method.call_with_flags` (verified live).
6. `secret-service` crate 3.0 — synchronous + async API, schema attributes:
   https://docs.rs/secret-service/3.0.1/secret_service/.
7. gtk4-rs 0.7 `gtk::DropDown` with `gio::ListStore` of `glib::Object` subclasses:
   https://gtk-rs.org/gtk4-rs/stable/0.7/docs/gtk4/struct.DropDown.html and the Gtk-rs book's "Custom List Models" chapter.
8. libadwaita 0.5 `adw::PreferencesWindow`, `adw::PasswordEntryRow`, `adw::ToastOverlay`:
   https://world.pages.gitlab.gnome.org/libadwaita/doc/1.4/.
9. NixOS manual — `security.polkit.extraConfig`, `users.groups`, `systemd.tmpfiles.rules`:
   https://nixos.org/manual/nixos/stable/options.html.

---

## 12. Open Questions / Decisions Locked in This Spec

- We **do not** introduce a long-running daemon. All privileged writes go through the short-lived `vex-vpn-helper` invoked via `pkexec`.
- We **keep** zbus pinned to 3.x; no 4.x patterns are introduced.
- We **keep** GTK4 calls strictly on the main thread; HTTP and D-Bus run on the existing Tokio runtime; results are delivered with `glib::spawn_future_local` as the file already does.
- We **do not** change the binary name `vex-vpn`; the helper is a *second* binary in the same crate (`vex-vpn-helper`), satisfying the project constraint that `vex-vpn` itself is unchanged.
